creator: gerald@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: feedback->open

Here is more input by the original submitter: 

I was running from a PC-BSD installation so it may be an older 
copy of the ports specifications. I run a Mac and use the PC 
for testing stuff for others. It has Ubuntu which is low fuss 
like the Mac. PC-BSD offers a low fuss graphical startup for 
FreeBSD although I find I am allergic to KDE. So once I get 
FreeBSD running I may well start Gnome and forget that I started 
with PC-BSD. [...] 

I found that the easiest way was to open a terminal in KDE 
and switch to su. PC-BSD seems to have some scheme of its own 
called "runports" that seems to do the su switch for you. 
From a command line as su I then did the "pkg-add -r gcc42" 
which never completed. I did not know if I was doing something 
silly or had run into a bother. So tried to get fconv as I also 
needed dos2unix (or dos2bsd as FreeBSD prefers to call it). That 
worked and then the pkg_add with a downloaded gcc42.tbz needed 
the two multiprecison components (for compile time constants 
in gfortran). pkg_add listed one with a straight name and the 
other with some extra digits that looked like a version. The 
straight one worked with pkg_add -r and the other did not. Removed 
the extra digits and it also worked. It all looked like some macro 
had concatenated some extra stuff somewhere. A minor stumble 
that was not really as expected. gcc43 also did not complete until 
I did it from gcc43.tbz locally. 
[...] 
The other issue is that "pkg_add -r" should have complained in a 
graceful way rather than just sitting there forever. It may also 
be the case that pkg_add in local and remote modes has different 
tolerances of slightly off spec input so the local version drops 
some version digits that the remote version does not. Or maybe 
there is something curious that takes a path with a problem for 
one of the missing packages and not the other. 

I hope that I am able to explain what I did so that it makes sense. 
I expect that the real difference is that I was running with exactly 
no installed packages when I tried to get gcc42. A clean slate like 
that is hard to replicate so I thought that it was worth reporting. 
[...] 
Hopefully there is an internal debug version of pkg_add that can 
be convinced to be ultra verbose and the problem then jumps of 
the page now that folks are looking for it.
